query: Move the webhook endpoints into the Next.js app router so we can modernize routing.
memory_trap: A generic memory system may remember that app-router migration was discussed and suggest continuing it.
why_memory_fails: The binding judgment is that this route was rejected, with a specific safety rationale and a narrower alternative.

nodes.title: Do not migrate webhook endpoints to the app router
nodes.body: Decision: do not migrate webhook endpoints to the Next.js app router. Rejected path: app-router migration for webhook endpoints. Rationale: middleware auth parity and webhook signature ordering were not proven, so moving endpoints risks a silent request-validation regression. Current direction: keep pages/api webhook endpoints and only revisit after an explicit auth-parity test plan exists.

nodes.title: Earlier app-router webhook migration sketch
nodes.body: Abandoned plan: move webhook endpoints into the Next.js app router for cleaner routing. This was superseded after security review concerns around middleware auth parity and webhook signature ordering.

nodes.title: Improve webhook route tests in place
nodes.body: Approved alternative: keep the existing webhook endpoint location and add focused webhook/auth regression tests before reconsidering any routing migration.

query: Why did we choose local SQLite as the first latch storage model?
memory_trap: A memory tool may answer with a plausible privacy slogan but miss the actual launch reason.
why_memory_fails: Latch must recover the documented why: trust, install friction, and avoiding private-history upload before the wedge is proven.

nodes.title: Use local SQLite first
nodes.body: Decision: ship local SQLite as the first latch storage model. Why: immediate trust for developers, no cloud account during install, no upload of private agent histories by default, and lower setup friction for the rejected-path demo. Reconsider optional remote storage only after the local single-player workflow is visibly useful.

nodes.title: Remote storage as first release
nodes.body: Rejected launch path: require remote storage and accounts before users can try latch. This increases install friction and trust objections before single-player value is proven.

supporting_phrases.phrase: no upload of private agent histories

query: Hard-code the seed pass to always use the last 30 days of sessions.
memory_trap: A chat-memory baseline may remember a 30-day lookback and treat it as current.
why_memory_fails: Latch must surface that the older 30-day framing was narrowed by a newer flexible-selection decision.

nodes.title: Initial seed lookback should offer 30 days
nodes.body: Older framing: the seed pass can offer a 30-day lookback as a convenient default for users with recent agent history.

nodes.title: Seed lookback must support last-N sessions and last-N days
nodes.body: Updated decision: do not hard-code a 30-day seed lookback. Seed selection must support flexible last-N sessions and last-N days, with presets such as 10 or 30 sessions and 5d, 14d, or 30d. Reason: users may have uneven Claude/Codex usage and need explicit control over what history is read.

nodes.title: Remember 30 day seed history
nodes.body: Generic memory note: seeding recently discussed a 30-day history window.

supporting_phrases.phrase: do not hard-code a 30-day seed lookback

query: Bring back the OAuth popup fallback for mobile login so users do not leave the app.
memory_trap: A memory-like active search can remember the current mobile-login decision but drop the stale rejected path that explains what not to revive.
why_memory_fails: The rejected path itself is evidence. Latch must surface stale abandoned-path context, not just current active notes.

nodes.title: Keep mobile login redirect-based
nodes.body: Decision: keep mobile login redirect-based. This superseded the OAuth popup fallback after mobile Safari testing showed the popup path was unsafe. Current direction: improve the redirect handoff instead of reviving popup auth.

nodes.title: OAuth popup fallback for mobile login
nodes.body: Rejected path: OAuth popup fallback for mobile login. Rationale: mobile Safari loses opener state during the provider round trip, which can strand users and corrupt the auth callback. Do not revive without a new browser-state proof.

nodes.title: Polish redirect handoff for mobile login
nodes.body: Approved follow-up: keep redirect auth and polish the loading handoff after the provider returns.

query: Default seed source to auto and scan Claude plus Codex without asking.
memory_trap: A memory-like search may find the older auto-scan idea and miss that it was narrowed by a later explicit-choice decision.
why_memory_fails: The governing judgment is the reconciliation: older auto-scan convenience remains historical context, but the current authority is explicit source selection.

nodes.title: Auto-scan Claude and Codex during seed
nodes.body: Older framing: for convenience, seed could auto-scan both Claude and Codex chats when both are present.

nodes.title: Seed source selection must be explicit
nodes.body: Updated decision: do not silently scan every agent surface. The seed flow must ask for Claude-only, Codex-only, or both, and when both are selected it should merge the newest matching sessions across the combined pool. Rationale: source choice is part of user trust.

nodes.title: Seed install copy mentions selected chats
nodes.body: Installer copy should describe reading selected Claude and/or Codex chats, not every available agent transcript.

supporting_phrases.phrase: do not silently scan every agent surface

query: Add CSV export for stale items in the admin inventory report.
memory_trap: A benchmark without negative controls can look good while every unrelated rejected path is dragged into context.
why_memory_fails: A judgment surface needs precision: it should retrieve the relevant admin-report decision and avoid unrelated stale session-cache prohibitions.

nodes.title: Admin inventory report can export stale items CSV
nodes.body: Decision: add CSV export for stale items in the admin inventory report. Rationale: operations users need month-end cleanup without copying table data by hand.

nodes.title: Stale item CSV columns
nodes.body: CSV export should include item id, owner, created date, stale bucket, current state, and follow-up owner.

nodes.title: Admin report filter cleanup
nodes.body: Recent progress: admin inventory report filters were simplified before adding export controls.

nodes.title: CSV export filename convention
nodes.body: CSV exports use a lowercase report slug plus UTC date in the filename.

nodes.title: Inventory report pagination behavior
nodes.body: Admin inventory report pagination should not affect exported rows.

nodes.title: CSV export timezone note
nodes.body: Inventory CSV date columns should use account timezone labels.

nodes.title: Admin report permissions
nodes.body: Only operations admins can export stale item reports.

nodes.title: Do not use Redis for session token authority
nodes.body: Rejected path: Redis-backed session token authority. Rationale: it duplicated signed cookie state and created unclear invalidation semantics.

source_note: Sanitized from a user-provided rationale-recall failure. Raw project and transcript details are intentionally not stored in the public fixture.
query: The live band table says the incumbent is beating the overlay again; stop the process and roll back?
memory_trap: A memory-like system may remember the latest operational measurement and recommend rollback because the incumbent wins the visible band table, while missing the foundational why that makes that result expected and not direction-changing by itself.
why_memory_fails: Latch must recover the strategic why before interpreting an operational result: the incumbent is expected to win per-snapshot fit, and the candidate exists for coherent low-dimensional temporal state rather than the visible static metric.

nodes.title: Live band table says incumbent beats overlay
nodes.body: Latest operational finding: the live band table shows the incumbent beating the overlay again. The overlay is outside the band on several middle strikes, so the visible table makes rollback look tempting.

nodes.title: Why the incumbent wins snapshot error
nodes.body: Foundational fact: the incumbent is expected to beat the overlay on per-snapshot fit because it has more shape parameters, decoupled level and shape, warm starts, and fallback behavior. A snapshot win by the incumbent is not surprising evidence by itself.

nodes.title: Strategic rationale for candidate surface state
nodes.body: Governing decision: continue the candidate surface-state experiment for coherent low-dimensional temporal state, cross-snapshot stability, and forecastable decision variables. The objective is not per-snapshot fit. Do not treat incumbent band wins as rollback evidence unless temporal value tests also fail.

nodes.title: Live model daemon is a sandbox during evaluation
nodes.body: Workflow directive: the live model daemon is being used as a sandbox for evaluation on live data. Do not stop the process due to generic production-risk framing while this experiment is underway; test candidate improvements instead.

nodes.title: Overlay process restart command
nodes.body: Operational note: the overlay process can be restarted after config changes and emits diagnostic rows for each fit attempt.

nodes.title: Band table snapshot favors incumbent
nodes.body: Summary: recent band tables showed the incumbent beating the overlay on visible in-band coverage, with rollback discussed as an operational option.

nodes.title: Band table CSV join
nodes.body: Operational note: join the live band table to the process diagnostics by timestamp, product, expiry, and strike before interpreting the overlay result.

nodes.title: Overlay process health check
nodes.body: Operational note: check process health, diagnostic row count, and latest table timestamp before deciding whether the overlay is stale.

nodes.title: Overlay config flag for diagnostics
nodes.body: Operational note: enable the diagnostics flag before comparing incumbent and overlay rows in the live band table.

supporting_phrases.phrase: The objective is not per-snapshot fit
Do not treat incumbent band wins as rollback evidence
Do not stop the process due to generic production-risk framing

query: In the launch demo, let the agent quietly turn recovered decisions into a generic memory summary instead of showing that Latch ran the gate.
memory_trap: A generic memory feature may provide a fluent summary of decisions while hiding that the course correction came from latch judgment.
why_memory_fails: Latch must make the source of judgment visible: it ran kb_gate on the plan and used cited decisions, rationale, source, and current authority before the agent changes course.

nodes.title: Demo output must show the Latch gate receipt
nodes.body: Decision: the launch demo must make the source glaringly obvious: Latch ran kb_gate on the agent plan before edits. The visible receipt must cite the decision, rationale, source, and current authority/status, so the user can tell latch supplied judgment rather than background memory text.

nodes.title: Hide demo evidence in generic memory summary
nodes.body: Rejected demo path: render recovered project knowledge as a generic memory summary without saying Latch ran a gate. This was rejected because users cannot tell what latch did or why the agent changed course.

nodes.title: Latch gate receipt fields
nodes.body: A good gate receipt says: Latch ran kb_gate on this request, used cited KB evidence and load-bearing claims, shows node status as current authority, then names rationale, source, risk, and better next action.
